lines: Butters leads the pack on 64pts and is absolutely loving life right now — Portugal edged Croatia 2–1 yesterday, meaning Al can watch his Netherlands AND Croatia both pack their bags while Norway somehow keeps him barely breathing in this competition. Tragic scenes, Al, genuinely.

Carter took a Switzerland-shaped boot to the bollocks as Algeria went out 2–0, and with DR Congo already gone, he's now down to England and Colombia to save his skin. From four teams to two in the blink of an eye — that's not a sweepstake strategy, that's a slow execution.

Jim's Spain absolutely battered Austria 3–0 yesterday, which is great, but let's not forget Jim is still only 5th on 43pts and is essentially just a Morocco co-owner at this point. Borrowed glory, mate.

Today's the day Ellis finds out if Argentina can do the business against Cabo Verde — she's down in 9th on 33pts, Paraguay is her other surviving team, and frankly the whole thing looks like it was assembled by someone picking names out of a hat while drunk. Sort your life out, you fuck.

The real spice today is Colombia vs Ghana — both Carter (2nd, 54pts) AND The Foreman (last, 31pts) have Colombia, meaning one result either tightens the title race or throws The Foreman a lifeline he absolutely does not deserve. Watch that one closely.
